criteria.1: Depicts the initial setup with a visible partition dividing the container and gas confined to one side.
criteria.2: Displays only the entities specified in the prompt (container, partition, gas) as visually distinct throughout the video.
criteria.3: Shows the removal of the partition as a clearly observable mechanical event.
criteria.4: Maintains the initial spatial relation of gas occupying only one compartment until the partition is removed.

criteria.1: Shows the partition being removed before any visible gas movement or expansion occurs.
criteria.2: Depicts the gas remaining stationary until the partition is fully removed.
criteria.3: Displays the sequence of partition removal followed immediately by gas expansion in a continuous, unbroken timeline.

criteria.1: Depicts the gas expanding spontaneously into the available space after partition removal, without any external force or influence.
criteria.2: Shows the partition being removed by a physically plausible mechanical process, not by disappearance or non-physical means.
criteria.3: Maintains a constant total amount of gas throughout the video, with no loss or gain of gas.
criteria.4: Illustrates the gas reaching a uniform distribution in the container after expansion, with no persistent gradients, clumping, or segregation.
criteria.5: Avoids depicting the gas spontaneously returning to its original compartment or reversing the expansion process.

criteria.1: Visually demonstrates that removing the partition causes the gas to rapidly fill the entire container.
criteria.2: Depicts the gas occupying the entire container at the end of the process, leaving no empty compartment.
criteria.3: Shows the expansion as a spontaneous and rapid process, not gradual or externally forced.

criteria.1: Maintains the visual identity and appearance of the gas, partition, and container throughout the video.
criteria.2: Keeps the location and boundaries of all entities continuous, with no unexpected changes or discontinuities.
criteria.3: Displays all actions and events without visual or logical discontinuities, such as objects teleporting or changing form.

criteria.1: Excludes any visual cues or actions suggesting external intervention in the gas expansion process.
criteria.2: Avoids showing the partition being removed by fading out, vanishing, or any non-mechanical means.
criteria.3: Does not depict non-uniform gas distribution after expansion, such as persistent clumps or gradients.
criteria.4: Omits any visual elements or annotations that could imply incorrect causes or mechanisms for the gas expansion.
